title: I Want To Love It More
description:
No one said America was easy to love. Perhaps it would be easier to love if it were more peaceful and more fractal-like (as the drawing suggests). However we were not given this choice of America vs. Utopia ("no-place"). We were blessed with this complex world as it is, America and all.

Asking what one can do for their country (and vice-versa) is a very 1961 sentiment. Right now the question seems to be about how we will act together to bring about an effective, aligned, inclusive, and resilient future for America.

I venture that all those interested in a participatory and decentralized patriotism, will find plenty of work to be done in the coming weeks, months, and years. Our collective blood, sweat, and tears will produce new kaleidoscopic tie-dyes of Red, White, and Blue. We will transmute the atrocities and inadequacies of the past, into anti-fragile governance structures and more equitable economies, ecologies, and educational paradigms. 

We will expect America to behave according to the highest possible standard (American expectationalism), and never allow any exceptions to this rule (American anti-exceptionalism). Ad victoriam!
